message:
bd-3bxhj.3.4: Infer layout, focus, and interaction intent graphs

Adds intent_inference module that analyzes canonical MigrationIr to produce
confidence-scored intent graphs for high-quality FrankenTUI generation:

- Layout constraint graph: classifies view nodes into layout regions
  (linear, grid, stack, fixed, scrollable, leaf), infers flex/grid
  constraints from explicit StyleIntent and structural heuristics,
  detects app-level patterns (sidebar+main, header/content/footer,
  dashboard grid, tabbed interface, modal overlay)
- Focus traversal graph: identifies focusable nodes via 5 signals
  (user-input events, interactive elements, explicit focus order,
  ARIA roles, state-write-on-interaction), computes tab order
  respecting explicit focus_order then tree DFS, detects focus
  groups by common ancestor with modal focus trapping
- Interaction cluster graph: seeds from user-input events, follows
  state transitions and effect triggers to find connected subgraphs,
  classifies patterns (form submission, toggle, navigation, search,
  modal, pagination, selection), detects cross-cluster state flows

All inferences carry Confidence scores [0,1] with rationale strings
and Evidence chains linking back to specific IR nodes. Ambiguous
layout directions preserved as ranked alternatives for planner.

22 tests covering all three inference domains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
